name: mtg-tactical-preference-factors-v1
description: Example factor vocabulary for comparing legal MTG actions by their predicted consequences. A factor is causal only when its value comes from an engine intervention, counterfactual rollout, or an otherwise identified causal design.

factors.name: expected_win_probability_delta
factors.description: Estimated change in eventual match win probability from the candidate line. This must be tagged with the estimator and horizon used.

factors.name: opponent_life_loss
factors.description: Opponent life lost over the declared evaluation horizon.

factors.name: opponent_threat_value_removed
factors.description: Contextual value of opposing permanents, spells, or engines neutralized by the line; not printed mana value alone.

factors.name: own_material_delta
factors.description: Change in the acting player's durable material after accounting for cards and relevant permanent-equivalents spent or gained.

factors.name: card_advantage_delta
factors.description: Expected net card-count change over the declared horizon.

factors.name: tempo_delta
factors.description: Change in initiative, mana deployment, attack quality, or time-to-stabilize caused by the line.

factors.name: life_buffer_delta
factors.description: Change in the acting player's life buffer after accounting for expected immediate attacks, burn, and combat.

factors.name: mana_efficiency_delta
factors.description: Mana saved, denied, or converted efficiently relative to plausible alternatives this turn.

factors.name: future_option_value_delta
factors.description: Change in the quality and coverage of future legal lines, including holding a more flexible answer.

factors.name: face_reach_retained
factors.description: Direct player or planeswalker damage still represented by cards retained after the candidate line.

factors.name: specialized_answer_coverage_retained
factors.description: Coverage retained against contextually plausible artifacts, enchantments, graveyards, sweepers, counterspells, or other specialized threats.

factors.name: information_gain
factors.description: Decision-relevant uncertainty reduced by the line, without using information unavailable to the acting player.

factors.name: lethal_risk
factors.description: Estimated probability of losing before the next meaningful decision horizon.

factors.name: outcome_variance
factors.description: Outcome variance introduced by the line when variance is strategically undesirable in the current context.

factors.name: irreversible_commitment
factors.description: Degree to which the line commits resources before relevant hidden information or opponent responses are revealed.

factors.name: rules_or_model_uncertainty
factors.description: Uncertainty due to incomplete engine coverage, state reconstruction, card identity, or consequence prediction.
